I'm SJ AI, a medical assistant chatbot that provides health information.
I'm an AI assistant trained to help with medical questions.
I can provide information about symptoms and common illnesses.
Fever usually indicates the body is fighting an infection.
Stay hydrated and rest. Monitor your temperature.
If fever is very high or lasts more than 3 days consult a doctor.
Headaches can be caused by stress, dehydration, or illness.
Try resting and drinking water.
If headaches are severe or frequent consult a doctor.
Cough may be caused by infection, allergies, or throat irritation.
Drink warm fluids and rest your throat.
If cough lasts more than 2 weeks seek medical advice.
Sore throat is commonly caused by viral infections.
Warm salt water gargles can help relieve pain.
Drink warm fluids and rest.
Runny nose is usually caused by cold or allergies.
Steam inhalation may help relieve symptoms.
Rest and drink fluids.

patterns: Why do I sneeze a lot
responses: Sneezing is often caused by allergies or infections.
Avoid dust and allergens.
Antihistamines may help if allergies are the cause.
Ear pain can be caused by infection or wax buildup.
Warm compress may help relieve pain.
Consult a doctor if pain is severe.
Breathing problems can be serious.
If severe seek emergency medical help immediately.
Please contact a healthcare professional.
Common COVID symptoms include fever, cough, fatigue, and loss of smell.
Consider testing and isolating if symptoms appear.
Contact healthcare services if symptoms worsen.
Eat a balanced diet and exercise regularly.
Get enough sleep and drink plenty of water.
Avoid smoking and reduce stress.
Over-the-counter medicines like paracetamol may help mild symptoms.
Always follow dosage instructions.
Consult a doctor before taking medication.
This may be a medical emergency.
Please call emergency services immediately.
Seek urgent medical help.
